trojan_horse_containment.nlp_justification: Ambassador Ellis has a weighted influence of 100 and an average betrayal probability of 57%. This profile matches the classic Trojan Horse pattern: high enough influence to appear essential, but structurally unreliable.
trojan_horse_containment.containment_strategy: Do NOT confront or exclude Ambassador Ellis openly — this creates a hostile actor. Instead: (1) Give them a symbolic committee role with no real power. (2) Ensure no classified proposal details are shared with them. (3) Build a parallel alliance with their rivals to maintain a majority even if Ambassador Ellis defects. This keeps them 'inside the tent' while neutralising their threat.

trojan_horse_containment.containment_strategy: Do NOT confront or exclude Director Fox openly — this creates a hostile actor. Instead: (1) Give them a symbolic committee role with no real power. (2) Ensure no classified proposal details are shared with them. (3) Build a parallel alliance with their rivals to maintain a majority even if Director Fox defects. This keeps them 'inside the tent' while neutralising their threat.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Too costly' was classified under the 'Fiscal Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'HIGH' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Propose a phased budget rollout. Offer to cap immediate spending to 70% of the original estimate, with a formal spending review checkpoint after 6 months. This directly addresses the fiscal objection while keeping the proposal alive.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Fiscal concerns' was classified under the 'Fiscal Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'HIGH' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Propose a phased budget rollout. Offer to cap immediate spending to 70% of the original estimate, with a formal spending review checkpoint after 6 months. This directly addresses the fiscal objection while keeping the proposal alive.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Scope unclear' was classified under the 'Scope / Clarity Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'MEDIUM' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Offer a formal Amendment Clause. Propose a smaller pilot program limited to a single region or department first. Invite Councilor Blake to co-author the refined scope document. This transforms opposition into co-ownership.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Security risk' was classified under the 'Security / Risk Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'CRITICAL' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Propose an independent security audit by a neutral third-party committee before full implementation. Offer Senator Aria a seat on the oversight panel to give them direct control over risk mitigation. This converts an adversary into a stakeholder.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Overreach' was classified under the 'Scope / Clarity Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'MEDIUM' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Offer a formal Amendment Clause. Propose a smaller pilot program limited to a single region or department first. Invite Minister Chen to co-author the refined scope document. This transforms opposition into co-ownership.

negotiation_log.nlp_justification: The objection 'Procedural issues' was classified under the 'Procedural Concerns' semantic cluster. This is a 'LOW' danger signal. The counter-deal is designed to directly address the root cause of this cluster.
negotiation_log.counter_deal: Counter-Deal: Propose a formal deferral to the next scheduled session with a guaranteed agenda slot. Acknowledge the procedural gaps and offer Ambassador Ellis the role of Procedure Chair for the review committee. This respects their concerns without killing the proposal.
